change: ICT decision logging: log real commitment_score (None when the sovereign CommitmentDetector did not run, which is ALWAYS in the live ict.orchestrator path because the bridge is orphaned) instead of the constant size-multiplier 1.0; log the size multiplier under its own field commitment_size_mult; made validation_cycle + harvest_cycle None-safe.
rationale: RQ-REST-018 (verified REST-029): commitment_score was logged as a constant 1.0 in 100% of entries, conflating the size multiplier with the price-action quality score and biasing Oracle toward 'fully committed'. Honest None removes the misinformation. Isolation-safe (no new sovereign import; uses existing importlib hook). No change to entries/exits/sizing. Verified: imports OK, isolation test passes, no regression on test_ict_pipeline.py (17/17 of the passing subset unchanged).
deferred_followup: Wiring the real sovereign CommitmentDetector into the LIVE ict.orchestrator path is a separate ARCHITECTURAL decision (the ict-engine bridge that was meant to inject it is orphaned; the live scanner runs `python -m ict.orchestrator`). Requires Colin sign-off because it changes the live scanner entry point / resurrects the bridge.

old: flat 0.10 band on |open/prev_close-1| and |close/open-1|, no tiering, no time-of-day doubling
new: tiered Reg NMS LULD via backtester/luld.py: Tier1 5% / Tier2 10% for px>=$3.00, 20% for $0.75<=px<$3.00, lesser of 75% or $0.15 below $0.75; DOUBLED 09:30-09:45 and 15:35-16:00; gap_before (missing RTH minute) retained unchanged
rationale: The flat 10% band ignored security tier and the opening-period doubling. Every gapper in this universe is a Tier 2 name entered inside the 09:30-09:45 doubling window, where the correct band is 20% (or more for sub-$3 names), not 10%. Spuriously flagged bars were charged HALT_RESUME_SLIP=0.02, biasing backtests PESSIMISTIC. MEASURED on 60 random events from data/research/gapper/per_candidate_enriched.csv (seed 20260718): at the 09:31 HYP-107 entry bar the old rule flagged 2/56 = 3.6% of events vs 0/56 = 0.0% under the tiered rule, i.e. 0.071% average spurious cost removed per trade. At the 10:30 HYP-093 entry bar: 0/59 under both rules, no measurable effect. Across the full 09:30-09:45 window (n=40 events): old 4.30% of minutes flagged vs new 2.32%, so 46% of the old rule's opening-window flags were false positives. This is a small correctness fix (~7bp on the long leg, ~0 on the short), NOT a result-changing one. An earlier draft of this change claimed 'nearly every opening minute' and '~2% per trade'; both were asserted from plausibility rather than measurement and were wrong.
direction: UPWARD — corrected backtests will show slightly HIGHER net returns than before. Stated explicitly and logged BEFORE regenerating any numbers, because an upward correction is where motivated reasoning enters.
affects: all realistic_fills-based backtests prior to this commit (HYP-093, HYP-105/106/107 fill modelling)
action_required: Re-run affected backtests. Prior net-return figures are stale and biased LOW by roughly 7bp on long-leg gapper trades; short-leg figures are unaffected.
ground_truth_coverage: NASDAQ halt snapshots exist for 5 sessions only (2026-07-14..18). No precision/recall claim is made from that sample.

param: SCENARIOS spread model -> MEASURED_SPREAD fitted model
old: k_range*bar_range + k_illiq/sqrt($vol), clipped to scenario floor/cap (base cap 0.08 round-trip); docstring asserted real spreads are 1-15%
new: log(half_spread) = 0.324468 + 0.193418*log(price) - 0.383361*log(minute_$vol) + 0.368907*log(bar_range), clipped to observed p1=0.00009 / p99=0.02707
rationale: The 1-15% spread assumption was never measured and is wrong. 313 real NBBO observations at the frozen 09:31 entry instant (MINING-period events only, holdout untouched) give p10 0.13% / median 0.55% / p90 2.06% / p99 5.01%. At the entry leg the legacy model charged a median 6.2060% round-trip against a measured 0.5510% -- an 11.3x overcharge, saturating its 8% cap on gapper opens. New model R2(log)=0.404, resid sd 0.878; dominant term is log_dollar_vol at -0.383 (more liquidity, tighter spread), economically correct. Caps are observed p1/p99 percentiles, not hand-picked, so wide events are still charged up to 5.41% round-trip. Single-event A/B (CRE 2026-06-16): real quotes +0.24%, new model -0.73%, legacy -7.20%.
direction: UPWARD -- corrected backtests show HIGHER net returns. On a reconstructed HYP-107-like set the legacy model produced median -1.90% where measured spreads give +2.26%: a sign flip. Logged BEFORE regenerating downstream numbers.
affects: ALL realistic_fills-based backtests prior to this commit -- HYP-093, HYP-105/106/107, any gapper cost modelling
action_required: Re-run affected backtests with the measured model. Prior net figures are biased LOW by several pp/trade on gapper events.
caveat: This does NOT change any verdict. Constitutional yield on the reconstructed set is 0.0366%/day (x notional) vs the 0.05%/day floor -- still BELOW. Spread was never the binding constraint; sizing x trade frequency is.

6h tripped false-STALE against a 7.5h-old same-day forex_proximity.json scan, masking the substantive NARROWING classifier (both give STAND_ASIDE, but STALE hides the real reason). forex_proximity is scanned a few times daily; 12h uniform with es_nq/macro avoids false-stale between scans while still forcing STAND_ASIDE on genuinely dead data. Direction is not risk-loosening: verdict remains STAND_ASIDE today.

change: ADD platform.health {freshness_hours{shadow:96,gauntlet:720,regime:1.5}, contract_max_age_hours:1.5, edge_health{n_needed_default:250, divergence_R_threshold:0.5}, consecutive_breaker_halt_n:2}
rationale: New conscience/measurement layer (specs/measurement_layer.md) needs staleness limits for the shadow ledger + gauntlet backtest + regime contract it reads, an honest n_needed floor so an edge cannot read healthy from a handful of shadow signals, an edge-divergence threshold, and the consecutive-breaker count that forces a HALT. These are MEASUREMENT thresholds, NOT risk caps — the drawdown ladder itself is read from RISK_CONSTITUTION.md Art.3, not duplicated. Fail-safe direction: every new default makes the kill switch MORE conservative (missing/thin data -> REDUCE/HALT, never TRADE). Logged before use per CLAUDE.md NON-NEGOTIABLE #4.
